2604.25477.title: DDA-Thinker: Decoupled Dual-Atomic Reinforcement Learning for Reasoning-Driven Image Editing
2604.25477.paper_summary: Recent image editing models have achieved strong visual fidelity but often struggle with tasks requiring complex reasoning. To investigate and enhance the reasoning-grounded planning for image editing, we propose DDA-Thinker, a Thinker-centric framework designed for the independent optimization of a planning module (Thinker) over a fixed generative model (Editor). This decoupled Thinker-centric paradigm facilitates a controlled analysis of the planning module and makes its contribution under a fixed Editor easier to assess. To effectively guide this Thinker, we introduce a dual-atomic reinforcement learning framework. This framework decomposes feedback into two distinct atomic rewards implemented through verifiable checklists: a cognitive-atomic reward to directly assess the quality of the Thinker's executable plan, which serves as the actionable outcome of the Thinker's reasoning, and a visual-atomic reward to assess the final image quality. To improve checklist quality, our checklist synthesis is grounded not only in the source image and user instruction but also in a rational reference description of the ideal post-edit scene. To support this training, we further develop a two-stage data curation pipeline that first synthesizes a diverse and reasoning-focused dataset, then applies difficulty-aware refinement to curate an effective training curriculum for reinforcement learning. Extensive experiments on reasoning-driven image editing benchmarks, including RISE-Bench and KRIS-Bench, demonstrate that our approach substantially improves overall performance. Our method enables a community model to achieve results competitive with strong proprietary models, highlighting the practical potential of Thinker-centric optimization under a fixed-editor setting.
2604.25477.paper_summary_zh: 最近的圖像編輯模型已經實現了強大的視覺真實性，但在需要複雜推理的任務中經常面臨挑戰。為了調查和增強基於推理的圖像編輯規劃，我們提出了DDA-Thinker，一個以Thinker為中心的框架，旨在對固定生成模型（Editor）上的規劃模塊（Thinker）進行獨立優化。這種解耦的以Thinker為中心的範式促進了對規劃模塊的受控分析，並使其在固定Editor下的貢獻更易於評估。為了有效指導這個Thinker，我們引入了一個雙原子強化學習框架。這個框架將反饋分解為兩個不同的原子獎勵，通過可驗證的檢查清單實施：一個認知原子獎勵，用於直接評估Thinker可執行計劃的質量，這是Thinker推理的可行結果，另一個視覺原子獎勵用於評估最終圖像質量。為了提高檢查清單的質量，我們的檢查清單合成不僅基於源圖像和用戶指令，還基於理想後編輯場景的合理參考描述。為了支持這一訓練，我們進一步開發了一個兩階段數據策展管道，首先合成一個多樣化且以推理為重點的數據集，然後應用難度感知的精煉來策劃有效的強化學習訓練課程。在基於推理的圖像編輯基準上進行的廣泛實驗，包括RISE-Bench和KRIS-Bench，顯示我們的方法顯著提高了整體性能。我們的方法使社區模型能夠達到與強大的專有模型競爭的結果，突顯了在固定編輯器設置下以Thinker為中心的優化的實際潛力。
2604.25477.author: Hanqing Yang et.al.
2604.25477.authors: Hanqing Yang, Qiang Zhou, Yongchao Du, Sashuai Zhou, Zhibin Wang, Jun Song, Tiezheng Ge, Cheng Yu, Bo Zheng
